rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope; do not introduce new architecture.
Move implementation-level bindings from component type annexes to implementation annexes when needed.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE expressions: Boolean variables must have Boolean expressions, numeric variables numeric expressions.
Delete invalid AGREE clauses that reference unresolved identifiers instead of adding missing architecture.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reference only identifiers visible in their current scope.
Generated AGREE code should preserve type consistency and avoid invalid helper declarations.
Validator diagnostics pointing to implementation-level issues in type annexes should be resolved by moving or removing clauses.
Package-qualified identifiers in AGREE must match imports and available model units.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions/guarantees; implementation annexes for bindings.
Helper functions and constants must be declared with valid AGREE scalar types: int, real, bool.
Expressions in AGREE must maintain type consistency across left and right sides of assignments.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to resolve missing identifiers by inventing new declarations or architecture.
Do not place implementation-level references in component type annexes unless they are valid variables or outputs.
Do not assume that unresolved references can be fixed by adding imports or package qualifiers without verifying model availability.
Do not generate AGREE expressions with mismatched types such as assigning a bool to an int variable.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics (specific to other components)
Model Analyst Agent's role in extracting AADL structure
Requirement Analyst Agent's role in compact requirement semantics
